Presence of Issues\n- All reports agree that the code is a straightforward array sorting function.\n- The code sorts an array numerically in ascending order using `a - b`.\n- No anomalies, obfuscation, or suspicious behavior are identified.\n- The source map comment is standard and does not imply malicious intent.\n\n**Conclusion:** The code is correct and performs a basic, legitimate sorting operation.\n\n---\n\n### 2. Errors, Flaws, or Mistakes in the Reports\n- The reports are consistent; they all recognize the code as a simple, standard sorting function.\n- No logical errors or security flaws are identified.\n- No report claims or implies malicious behavior or vulnerabilities.\n\n**Note:** The reports do not mention any potential issues like input validation, which could be relevant if the input is not guaranteed to be an array of numbers. However, given the context, this is a standard utility function.\n\n---\n\n### 3.
